steps.title: Isolate the square root term
steps.content:
Start with $p=\sqrt{12c}-25$. Add 25 to both sides to isolate the square root:
$$p + 25 = \sqrt{12c}$$

steps.title: Square both sides and solve for c
steps.content:
Square both sides to eliminate the square root:
$$(p + 25)^2 = 12c$$
Divide both sides by 12:
$$c = \frac{(p+25)^2}{12}$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.
